post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 200: fdas's All Stars
Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs. Do not refer to private conversations with the moderator in any way unless given permission.
    • 1.5: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.6: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.7: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.8: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.9: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.10: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.11: Confirm by saying what your favorite role from one of my games is.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and Vote: fDAs is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, Vote:fdas, Vote: fads, Vote: fdsa, and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 504 hours (3 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 504 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 120 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I, fdas am the moderator of Mafia Game 200.
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.8: Because vote modifying roles may exist, votecounts will only list who is voting who and will not give the number of votes on each player.
    • 5.9: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.10: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

17 individuals appeared in a large pavilion. A bright light shone around them, masking their forms and making each appear as though they were an ordinary individual. As they looked around at each other in confusion, fdas spoke from atop the dais some distance away.

"Welcome," fdas said. "You have been taken from disparate worlds, all with their unique characteristics and individuals. Out of everyone, you seventeen have been chosen to take place in this contest. I have already placed you in your teams. You all have your own abilities and powers, but you will need to rely on others. But be careful who you rely on. I shall only give you one tip, trust no one."

Then, he vanished, leaving the chosen behind to determine their fate.


Do not post until this line is crossed out

Day 1 begins. Current time: 20:00:00 EDT 16 April 2020.

post_rendered:
With regards to these tonereads, please note that I am not a significant proponent of the toneread to begin with. I am merely proposing these thoughts so as to start getting us out of RVS and creating meaningful discussion. Note that I fully dislike the use of tonereads in late D1 or any following days, so this is just an early D1 sort of thing.

Our Actions
For now, I think it is best to come up with tone reads (I don't like them, but what better thing can we do; RVS doesn't get us anywhere for the most part) and assess reactions, thereby creating meaningful discussion. By late D1 or early D2, I think we should discuss fdas's power roles a bit, because if roles such as the Professional Jobber are involved, inter-player dynamics and nk analysis will be largely contingent on the presence of certain power roles. Like tonereads, I don't really like PR analysis/hunting, but given as some crazy roles will likely be involved in this game, power roles are just something good to keep in mind, while not necessarily focusing to heavily on them.

Thoughts on Charge
To be honest, Charge has opened in a way that seems more like mathjay with good grammar. Not necessarily alignment indicative, but something interesting to think about as Charge makes more posts.

Thoughts on mathjay
Although mathjay has only made one post, I have found his/her lack of participation in RVS interesting. Granted, mathjay might just not have been online, but that still seems unusual. Like Charge, there is so little evidence that we can't be sure, but it is something to remember as the game continues. Significant hesitancy by mathjay to participate in discussion would be characteristic of a continuation of mathjay's opening.

Thoughts on BZ1
I don't know anything about BZ1's playstyle, but his/her opening has seemed quite towny. One exception—when BZ1 stated "i doubt that i am pr", I found that statement rather odd. Would you mind explaining further, BZ1?

Thoughts on avanders
avanders seemed towny in the first few posts, but he/she seemed serious in post #33, which concerns me. avanders seemed to seriously scumread Charge for not posting "reads", but have any of us (avanders included) really posted a serious read? I would townread avanders, but this post seems weird. Can you please explain if you meant post #33 seriously or not, and maybe your underlying thoughts behind that post, avanders?

NDMath
NDMath has seemed very towny, but this could very well be a well-crafted facade. Still, he/she is a townread for me.

With regards to some of the super-experienced players, including NDMath, most of them are such experts at appearing town that tone will not be enough to make solid reads on them. As a result, we should probably focus on some of the less-experienced players, and then analyze some of the extremely experienced players either later in D1 or through night action analysis. What are your thoughts on this course of action? Thank you very much!

Signed,
cw357

post_rendered:
Disclaimer: I know setup speculation is bad, but there were just some interesting things I was thinking about.
Setup speculation
So normally many games have 3p and I think it's highly likely that there's a 3p this game too.
I'm just going to guess SK to start with because that's one of the most common 3ps, and I find it also harder to read.

So I heard from a long-time SK player that SKs are usually either 1-Shot BP or Investigation Immune, the former being more common. And said person, often being 1-Shot BP SK, has had some intriguing thoughts on how to play 1-Shot BP SK - in acting towny, but not too towny; just enough to avoid suspicion, but not enough to be obvious NK target for Mafia. It seems that SK also kills town for the first few days, but if town is bad at scumhunting, would aim to kill mafia as Lylo nears.

I was thinking if there was some way to try and manipulate SK into killing Mafia, but such a plan would be complicated, and it's best to not overthink myself if SK doesn't even exist.

Now, because TTS Mafia exists, cult is also possible. I'm not quite sure how to deal with cult as my experience is sort of limited (185 was not super culty... I'm pretty sure cult was wiped out like in the first few days). I do recall GC and MY-2 apparently appearing culty in 185 so I would look at that I suppose.

Also, if cult exists, do not recruit me. Just don't. I'm not a fan of it.

Lastly, there are Jesters and [L-word]ees and [L-word]ers and Survivors, but I don't think they impact the game much. It's possible to joint win here and fdas doesn't end games on Jester hang.

It feels rather like we are being mislead from what ought to be our primary intention at the beginning of D1: trying to decide who the best D1 hang is. I fully understand that PRs, interactions, and any other mechanics are important to consider, but they are largely irrelevant until N1 actions have occurred. As a result, it seems like it makes the most since for us to analyze less-contributing players, because they would be better candidates for a D1 hang, owing to the fact that if we hang a significant contributor who flips town, we could lose important thoughts and ideas, while if a non-contributor hang flips town, we have not necessarily lost as much. If significant evidence against more active players (Charge, avanders, Kbwuf, NDMath, etc.) shows up in late Day 1, we can obviously reconsider, but for now, doesn't it make since to focus on some of our non-contributor players who have added little to the game? (kevinmathz, mathjay, hannahptl, etc.) As a result, I will be reading through the game (hopefully this evening) to try and read some of the less active players (hopefully without meta). Thank you very much!
Signed,
cw357

Alright, I am about to ISO some of the other suspicious semi-inactive players (e.g. Robotlover) but just wanted to say a few things about everything that has happened.

1) Please, please, please be careful with bandwagons. I get that early hangs probably won't happen, but depending on how many scum/3rd parties there are in this game, and there are probably quite a few third parties, we could end up in serious trouble.
2) If you are going to take the time to log on and read through the game, please try and post something insightful. Granted, commentary on the game is acceptable, but don't make that all you do. It gets us nowhere.
3) To the flakers, please try to make at least some effort. Honestly, probably one of the flakers is jester, but since not all of them are jester, it would be best if you could at least try to make some minimal contribution so that we know you are at least trying.

In summary, by being as upfront as possible, we can make scumhunting much more efficient in this game, and make up for the inactivity the game currently has. I'll be back soon with ISOs on all of my scumreads, at which point I'll try to do some analysis on them. See you then!

Signed,
cw357

Yikes, I've had so much going on lately that I totally forgot about Mafia, and here I am with what should be a PM prod, but fdas is inactive sooooo.......
Below are my thoughts on the game in general, my thoughts on lkarhart/Robotlover8 (the two inactives we've been quite suspicious of), midwaybear, kevinmathz, and mathjay, plus some further actions we can take to further scumhunting. (We have about one and one half weeks until deadline, and there's majority hang, so we have to come to a consensus at some point or another.)

P.S. I am compiling reads on these specific players because they are the players I have thoughts on; I don't really see the point of writing up a long list of players when I have no insightful thoughts on them. It's better to make detailed, insightful, reads on a few players than meaningless reads on them all.
Thoughts on lkarhart/Robotlover8
Robotlover8 hasn't made any more posts, but I was going through his/her past posts, and a lot of them seem fairly NAI. It doesn't look like Robot has played in a while, and is just trying to get a feel for where we should be going with the game. That being said, it could be subtle bandwagoning, so we have to be careful, although I am still more suspicious of lkarhat. Since I was last on, lkarhat made posts #364 and #366. #364 would be great if lkarhat was contributing more, but he/she has contributed so little that it seems to be an attempt to seem towny while not furthering the game. #366 justifies the lurking a little bit, since some of us just don't have as much to say, but lkarhat's attempts to justify bandwagoning seem extremely scummy.
lkarhat wrote:
-Bandwagoning. When there isn't any obvious arguments to make the best thing is to agree with other people's arguments if they make sense.
No, lkarhat. If there aren't any obvious arguments to make, you think of one, and tell us! If we stick to obvious arguments, it is an absolute disaster for town. That being said, in fdas games with majority hang, agreement can be important towards the end of D1, but for now, discussion of as many ideas as we can is what we MUST do. So if you are town and genuinely think that bandwagoning is a good idea, please do something else for the sake of furthering scumhunting. lkarhat is still my top scumread.

midwaybear
At first, I thought midway was a confirmed townie, owing to his/her consistent attempts at making and discussing reads. Now, I feel like midwaybear is just trying to mask fluff in seemingly relevant content. As a result, I am starting to feel that midwaybear is a strong scumlean for me, although not enough to vote him/her.

kevinmathz
kevinmathz is fairly null for me—I fully understand how his/her inactivity is NAI (I think I'll actually be doing his Lexington Math Tournament) but his/her recent statement that for him, "everybody is null" is really weird. Do you literally have no scumreads or townreads, or even leans??? But it certainly isn't enough for me to scumread kevinmathz.

mathjay
How is mathjay an SE? Honestly, I would think that mathjay is a jester, but it is probably not likely that a jester would be so blatantly antitown. You're not necessarily being scummy, but you are being the dictionary definition of antitown, so I sincerely hope a PR nks you. TROLLING IS NOT HELPFUL! We would rather you just not post and get replaced instead of trolling. But I just realized I'm basically copying @2above, so let me stop before I get sued for copyright violation.

Future Actions
I honestly feel like investigating midwaybear, and I will continue to think about lkarhat and Robotlover8, but I'd really like to see other people's top hang candidates so that I'm not just focusing on those three. We probably should narrow down who we want to hang to a few people within the next week or so, so please tell everyone who you think would be the best hang, and we can openly discuss thoughts on hanging those players.


Signed,
cw357

cw357 wrote:
With regards to these tonereads, please note that I am not a significant proponent of the toneread to begin with. I am merely proposing these thoughts so as to start getting us out of RVS and creating meaningful discussion. Note that I fully dislike the use of tonereads in late D1 or any following days, so this is just an early D1 sort of thing.

Our Actions
For now, I think it is best to come up with tone reads (I don't like them, but what better thing can we do; RVS doesn't get us anywhere for the most part) and assess reactions, thereby creating meaningful discussion. By late D1 or early D2, I think we should discuss fdas's power roles a bit, because if roles such as the Professional Jobber are involved, inter-player dynamics and nk analysis will be largely contingent on the presence of certain power roles. Like tonereads, I don't really like PR analysis/hunting, but given as some crazy roles will likely be involved in this game, power roles are just something good to keep in mind, while not necessarily focusing to heavily on them.

Thoughts on Charge
To be honest, Charge has opened in a way that seems more like mathjay with good grammar. Not necessarily alignment indicative, but something interesting to think about as Charge makes more posts.

Thoughts on mathjay
Although mathjay has only made one post, I have found his/her lack of participation in RVS interesting. Granted, mathjay might just not have been online, but that still seems unusual. Like Charge, there is so little evidence that we can't be sure, but it is something to remember as the game continues. Significant hesitancy by mathjay to participate in discussion would be characteristic of a continuation of mathjay's opening.

Thoughts on BZ1
I don't know anything about BZ1's playstyle, but his/her opening has seemed quite towny. One exception—when BZ1 stated "i doubt that i am pr", I found that statement rather odd. Would you mind explaining further, BZ1?

Thoughts on avanders
avanders seemed towny in the first few posts, but he/she seemed serious in post #33, which concerns me. avanders seemed to seriously scumread Charge for not posting "reads", but have any of us (avanders included) really posted a serious read? I would townread avanders, but this post seems weird. Can you please explain if you meant post #33 seriously or not, and maybe your underlying thoughts behind that post, avanders?

NDMath
NDMath has seemed very towny, but this could very well be a well-crafted facade. Still, he/she is a townread for me.

With regards to some of the super-experienced players, including NDMath, most of them are such experts at appearing town that tone will not be enough to make solid reads on them. As a result, we should probably focus on some of the less-experienced players, and then analyze some of the extremely experienced players either later in D1 or through night action analysis. What are your thoughts on this course of action? Thank you very much!

Signed,

End of Day Votecount:
mathjay (HANGED): olive0827, Made_in_2004, cw357, asdf334, midwaybear, bigmath, mathjay, Kbwuf
asdf334: snoozingnewt, kevinmathz


Once the day started, the players began deliberating among themselves, attempting to bring some order and rationality to the process. However, it soon descended into a true spectacle as fdas watched in amazement. It was times like these that reminded him of why creating these events was so fun and interesting, especially as he got to watch and know exactly what was truly happening. Kbwuf arose with a claim that he found out that mathjay was a 3p. How incredibly ironic, fdas thought as he saw Kbwuf push mathjay's wagon with such intensity. Finally, the conflict came to a head, and Kbwuf, leading the majority of the players confronted mathjay and demanded his death.

Kbwuf threw the oreo.

mathjay caught it.

“Death, by a poisoned oreo,” he said, quietly. “Poetic.”

Then he ate it.

“You remember the old saying,” he said after he had swallowed. “Fool me once, shame on you. Fool me twice, shame on me. Fool me thrice...? I was so nervous, at first. Surely, they’ll catch on, I worried. Surely, they’ll be smarter than this, I whined to fdas. Leave it up to me to job the easiest role in existence. But you... you have surpassed all my expectations.”

He began laughing. “Kbwuf, when you said you got a grey check, I was so glad NDMath died, because he would be rolling in his grave if he could see this. When you said that you knew grey was 3p because he was grey when he was Butcher, I was afraid, for a moment. But then you said you still wanted to kill me, so I decided to have a little fun. I spammed, I said anything and everything that popped into my head, I fluffed. And I asked you to kill me, and voted myself.”

“That,” he said with an evil laugh, “was when I realized you well and truly didn’t know. And it was so, so hard not to laugh, because it was so utterly ridiculous. But here I am, a dead man walking, and you, a living one in a grave. Fate is poetic, is she not?"

mathjay smirked.

“Congratulations,” mathjay said. “You played yourself.”

Then he died.

mathjay was The Butcher

Night 2 begins. Current time: 17:00:00 EDT 13 May 2020.

[b]lkarhat (HANGED):[/b] midwaybear, Kbwuf, bigmath, snoozingnewt[/color]

[color=#00f]As the day's deliberation's began, fdas stared at the proceedings with the look of incredulous amusement, as though he was watching a surreal comedy. While he certainly didn't expect careful and intellectual discussion, this was on another level. It was as though everyone was in a race to push someone else into the pit as fast as possible. And yet again the discussion ended abruptly. lkarhat was killed. He was a [/color][color=#9a00ff]Dark Archon[/color]

[color=#ff0000]Night 5 begins. Current time: 16:00:00 EDT 2 June 2020.[/color]

post_rendered:
"Congratulations to those that survived so far," fdas said. "It wasn't easy for any of you to make it this far. Many had fallen victim to the impulsive voters. While others fell to the machinations of those that slither in the night. Indeed, those who died tonight had not expected it."

"Now, I am sure you are all waiting to see the results of the night." fdas motioned to the first body. Kbwuf died. He was Yawen (Butcher II)

midwaybear died. He was an Infestor

"bigmath, Charge, snoozingnewt, you are the final three," fdas said. "You have made it this far, and victory is within your grasp. It will be up to you to take it."

Day 6 begins. Current time: 12:00:00 EDT 4 June 2020.

post_rendered:
To be quite frank, town did not deserve the win, but neither did mafia.

One huge problem I see with town is their tendency to believe claims at face value. These days, any random cat or dog could get out of being hanged by claiming a PR. This is frankly preposterous. Just because someone claimed a role doesn't mean they are town. On day 1, lkarhat was the top scumread before he claimed ghost, after which lkarhat suddenly became treated as conf town and the hang got pushed onto avanders who got hanged because he didn't decide to pull out some random claim like cop.

I see this a lot and it has always baffled me. Making a fake claim isn't hard and yet town seems to treat claims as gospel. Even when night actions look like this match up, like what kevin and midway claimed, it is entirely possible for the claims to be false, especially if the mafia make use of their own roles to emulate the roles they claim. Furthermore, investigative results can practically be invented by mafia since they already know alignments. Tracker results are more difficult, but still possible to fake. However, no one seemed to realize this. That lead to the situation this game where people thought a massclaim was a good idea. As you can see, it wasn't a good idea, and the only reason it didn't backfire harder was because the mafia screwed up big time.

Someone mentioned that there were a lot of cops in this game. That is true. However, mafia also had a lot of ways to deal with these cops, which they didn't use. Firstly, there was the godfather. If Florina had just roleclaimed VT at the start instead of competing for the rolecop claim, things would have been fine. The unconfirmed VT claim could be investigated and Florina would come up as town, thus securing another mafia as "conf town".

Next, midwaybear did not make effective use of his abilities. If he had actively used them instead of sitting on energy, he could have caused massive disruption to town. When a cop goes to investigate a player, midway can redirect the investigation to a mafia member, thus generating a fake redcheck. The town investigated would be mishanged and then the cop who had the fake result can then be hanged afterwards, leading to two mishangs and cop checks becoming discredited.

Now, despite the mafia screwups, town shot themselves in the foot with their second big mistake: quickhanging. Town was impulsive and did not take the time to fully analyze the information they had available. Thus, they did not see the very numerous mafia slips. For example, the redcheck on kevin quickly got pushed under the rug, and since kevin claimed everyone thought he was conf town. No one really thought too much about the suspicious investigation result that midway returned, and some research would have revealed that the result was impossible. By hanging so quickly, town let themselves be driven off a cliff by the mafia pulling the strings.

The lesson here is to treasure your discussion time and do not hammer until the deadline is close. You should make sure to take full advantage of the discussion time allocated for each day, and take the time to think and analyze. A lot of the errors in this game were due to lack of thinking, and if you took your time you would have been better at separating the lies from the truth.
